name: Sam Reynolds
title: Assistant Research Professor
bio: I am a biologist who has slowly migrated from cells, genes and proteins; to ecosystems, climate change and food security; to applied ecology; and finally, to conservation. I am now exploring how AI-enabled tools may dramatically alter evidence synthesis of grey and published literature to inform conservation action. I am also interested in AI applications in conservation more broadly, both in terms of how they work and how we implement them responsibly and ethically.

I hold a PhD in Zoology from the University of Cambridge (2021), which focused on harnessing and quantifying the impacts of aquatic invasive species, and an MSc (2013) and BSc (2011) in Biological sciences from the University of Warwick.
Beyond academia, I have worked for environmental NGOs (IUCN & ClientEarth), as a nature documentary researcher (National Geographic), and as an Environmental Consultant (WSP).

I am a faculty fellow for the Digital Statecraft Academy, a global hub for cultivating public leaders ready to navigate the complexities of data, AI and digital governance, and a founding member of Ai4Nature, a cross-sectoral initiative to advance responsible AI applications in biodiversity data and nature recovery.

education.degree: MSc in Environmental Bioscience in a Changing Climate
